footer.tagline: Free browser-based media tools. No uploads, no signups.

privacy.metaDescription: Learn how Media Toolbox protects your privacy. All file processing happens in your browser — nothing is uploaded to any server.
privacy.title: Privacy & Security
privacy.subtitle: Your files never leave your browser. We built Media Toolbox with privacy as the foundation, not an afterthought.
privacy.corePromiseBody: Every tool on Media Toolbox processes files entirely within your browser using client-side JavaScript and WebAssembly. Your files are never uploaded to any server, never stored anywhere, and never seen by anyone but you. When you close the tab, the data is gone.

step1.title: You select a file
step1.description: Your file is read directly by JavaScript in your browser. It never touches a network request.

step2.title: Processing happens locally
step2.description: All conversions, compressions, and edits run on your device using Web APIs, Canvas, WebAssembly (FFmpeg, PDF.js), and other browser technologies.

step3.title: You download the result
step3.description: The output is generated in-memory and offered as a download. No server is involved at any step.

noUpload.title: No File Uploads
noUpload.description: Files never leave your device. Processing uses your browser's built-in capabilities.

localStorage.title: No Persistent Storage
localStorage.description: We don't use cookies or local storage to track you. Your preferences (language, theme) are stored locally.

openSource.title: Verifiable Security
openSource.description: You can inspect network traffic in your browser's DevTools to confirm zero file uploads.

noLogs.title: No Server Logs
noLogs.description: Since everything runs client-side, there are no server logs of your file operations.

transparent.title: Fully Transparent
transparent.description: This is a static site hosted on Cloudflare Pages. No backend, no database, no API servers.

privacy.thirdPartyBody: Some tools load WebAssembly binaries (e.g., FFmpeg core) from CDNs like unpkg.com. These are open-source libraries fetched once and cached by your browser. No file data is sent to these CDNs — they only provide the processing code. We also use Google Analytics to collect anonymous usage statistics (page views, general location, browser type). No personally identifiable information is linked to your tool usage.

howItWorks.metaDescription: Learn how Media Toolbox processes your files entirely in the browser using JavaScript and WebAssembly. No uploads, no servers.
howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Three simple steps — your files stay on your device the entire time.

select.title: Pick Your File
select.description: Choose a file from your device or drag and drop it onto the tool. The file is read by JavaScript running inside your browser — it is never sent over the network.

process.title: Instant Local Processing
process.description: The tool processes your file right on your device using browser technologies like Canvas API, Web Audio API, and WebAssembly (FFmpeg, PDF.js, Tesseract). Processing speed depends on your device — most operations finish in seconds.

download.title: Download the Result
download.description: Once processing is complete, the output file is generated in memory and ready to download. Click the download button and save it anywhere on your device. No account or signup needed.

howItWorks.whyClientSideBody: Traditional online tools upload your files to a remote server, process them, and send the result back. This is slow, raises privacy concerns, and requires you to trust the service. Media Toolbox takes a different approach: every operation runs in your browser. Your files never leave your device, there are no upload size limits, and the tools work even on slow connections.

canvas.title: Canvas & Web APIs
canvas.description: Image operations like crop, resize, and format conversion use the browser's native Canvas API for fast, GPU-accelerated rendering.

wasm.title: WebAssembly
wasm.description: Video and audio tools use FFmpeg compiled to WebAssembly, running natively in the browser at near-native speed. PDF tools use PDF.js and pdf-lib.

webApis.title: Modern Web Standards
webApis.description: We leverage File API, Blob URLs, Web Workers, and other modern standards to handle large files efficiently without blocking the UI.

offline.title: Works Offline
offline.description: Since no server is involved, most tools continue to work even when your internet connection drops — as long as the page is already loaded.

howItWorks.privacyCta: Want to learn more about how we protect your data?
